title: Global Talent Acquisition Strategy & Governance Senior Manager - Temporary
required_skills: Content development for leadership meetings and townhalls
5+ years in HR, change management, or strategy consulting (preferred in global org)
bonus_skills: Experience in HR or talent acquisition functions
matched_skills: Programme governance & strategic workstream management: HSBC 6-workstream USD 8M programme coordination, risk escalation, prioritisation
Senior stakeholder management: C-suite reporting at HSBC, cross-functional stakeholder coordination at Hapag-Lloyd
Change management (partial): internal communications and change coordination for feature releases at Hapag-Lloyd
Internal communications: town hall events for 300+ at HSBC, company-wide newsletters and video production
Content development for leadership: executive sprint review reports, budget variance dashboards for C-suite
Cross-functional collaboration: coordinated Product, Engineering, Marketing, C-suite at HSBC
Agile sprint planning and delivery: Scrum ceremonies at Hapag-Lloyd
missing_skills: 5+ years of experience (candidate has ~4 years total; HSBC PMO role ~1.5 years is the strongest match)
Talent Acquisition / HR domain expertise (no direct TA or recruiting experience)
Strategy consulting background (no consulting firm experience)
Senior Manager-level autonomous leadership of large strategic programmes (candidate has been coordinator/PMO, not programme lead)
core_responsibilities: Lead high-impact strategic initiatives, managing workstreams and delivering content for senior decision forums
Lead change management efforts across TA function, manage change roadmap for major projects
Serve as trusted advisor to senior leadership, manage strategic interactions cross-functionally
Create compelling narratives and content for leadership meetings, townhalls, and external engagements
Drive internal communications for global TA initiatives, manage relationships with external partners/vendors
recommended_emphasis: Lead with HSBC programme governance: 6 workstreams, C-suite reporting, risk escalation — directly maps to the governance and strategic initiative management requirements
Emphasise the hybrid town hall for 300+ attendees and C-suite data storytelling as evidence of communications and content development capability
Position cross-functional coordination (Product/Engineering/Marketing/C-suite at HSBC) as stakeholder management at the required seniority level
Acknowledge the seniority gap honestly in cover letter; frame this as a step-up role with directly transferable governance and comms skills
summary: Match score 62.5% — moderate match. The candidate's HSBC PMO role maps well to programme governance, C-suite communications, and cross-functional stakeholder management that BCG requires. Key gaps are the 5+ years seniority bar, lack of TA/HR domain background, and no consulting firm experience. The temporary nature of the role makes it more accessible. Worth applying with a cover letter that clearly bridges PMO governance → TA governance and frames the communications track record as the primary value proposition.
